notices.title: Grantsville City Council Regular Meeting and Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE:

The Grantsville City Council will hold a Regular Meeting and Public Hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2026 in person at 429 East Main Street, Grantsville UT 84029 and electronically via Zoom beginning at 7:00 pm. 

The agenda is as follows: 

ROLL CALL 

AGENDA: 

1.	Public Comment
2.	Summary Action Items
     a.	Approval of Minutes from the April 1, 2026 City Council Regular Meeting
     b.	Approval of bills
3.	Consideration of Resolution 2026-24 approving the appointment of Candy Swallom to the Grantsville City Library Board
4.	Consideration of Resolution 2026-25 approving the appointment of Camille Coons to the Grantsville City Library Board
5.	Consideration of Resolution 2026-26 approving the Memorandum of Understanding with the Inland Port Authority
6.	Public Hearing Item: Discussion and consideration of adopting Resolution 2026-22 approving the expenditure of Class C Road Funds for road department equipment
7.	Public Hearing Item: Discussion and consideration of Ordinance 2026-16 approving and adopting an updated Annexation Policy Plan; repealing and replacing the prior Annexation Policy Plans; and providing for an effective date
8.	Consideration of Resolution 2026-23 approving the purchase of a sewer Vactor truck and authorizing partial funding and financing
9.	Consideration of Resolution 2026-21 approving the surplus of a decommissioned police department vehicle
10.	Presentation of a concept plan for the Sun Sage Terrace Subdivision, Phases 4 - 6
11.	Discussion and Council direction of the Townhomes on Willow sidewalks
12.	Discussion of a utility assistance program
13.	Adjourn

Alicia Fairbourne		
City Recorder

notices.title: Notice of Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
GRANTSVILLE CITY NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. for the purpose of receiving public input regarding a proposed budget amendment to the General Fund.

The proposed amendment will increase the Capital Projects line item within the Class C Roads Department budget in the amount of $3,037,000.00 for the purchase of road department equipment.

This amendment reflects the use of Class C Road Funds, which are restricted for roadway construction, maintenance, and improvement purposes. No new revenues or external funding sources are being proposed as part of this amendment.

The proposed equipment purchases are intended to improve operational efficiency, reduce long-term maintenance costs, and enhance the City's ability to maintain and preserve roadway infrastructure.

This meeting will be held electronically via Zoom and in-person at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville, Utah, 84029. Public comments are welcome and may be made in person or by participating through Zoom using the following:
Meeting URL: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4358843411?omn=89242001869 Dial-In: 1-253-215-8782 Meeting ID: 435 884 3411

Public comments may also be submitted via email to the City Recorder, Alicia Fairbourne, at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All comments will be distributed to the City Council and entered into the public record. Comments must be received prior to 12:00 p.m. (noon) on the day of the meeting in order to be considered.

Copies of the proposed resolution and supporting materials are available for public review at the Grantsville City Offices during regular business hours and as part of the City Council meeting materials posted on the City's official website and the Utah Public Notice Website.

DATED this 8th day of April, 2026

Alicia Fairbourne Grantsville City Recorder

notices.title: Notice of Public Hearing Annexation Policy Plan Amendment
notices.agenda_text:
Grantsville City Council
Notice of Public Hearing
Grantsville City Annexation Policy Plan Amendment

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that, in accordance with the provisions of Sections  §10-2-803 and §63G-30-102 of the Utah State Code, the Grantsville City Council will hold a discussion and public hearing on April 15, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville City, UT 84029. The meeting will also be broadcast electronically via Zoom. The purpose of the public hearing will be to receive public input and consider action on the proposed amendment to the Grantsville City Annexation Policy Plan.

You may view the meeting materials and agenda by visiting: https://www.grantsvilleut.gov/government/city_council.php 

Public comments regarding this matter are welcome and may be made by emailing Alicia Fairbourne, City Recorder, at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All emailed comments will be submitted to the legislative body and entered into the public record. Emailed comments must be received by 12:00 p.m. on the day of the meeting in order to be considered.

Additionally, comments may be made at the time of the meeting in person at Grantsville City Hall or by logging or dialing in to Zoom using the link below.

Dated this 1st day of April, 2026.


Alicia Fairbourne
Grantsville City Recorder



Join Zoom Meeting https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4358843411 

By Phone, Dial: 1-253-215-8782

Meeting ID: 435 884 3411

notices.title: Grantsville City Council and Planning Commission Special Work Meeting
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council and Grantsville City Planning Commission will hold a combined Work Meeting on Tuesday, April 14, 2026 in person at 429 East Main Street, Grantsville, UT 84029 and electronically via Zoom beginning at 7:00 pm. 

The agenda is as follows:

ROLL CALL

AGENDA ITEMS

1.	Presentation and Discussion: Discussion regarding the Grantsville City Master Transportation Plan, including current conditions, future transportation needs, and potential improvements. No formal action will be taken.

2.	Presentation and Discussion: Discussion of proposed amendments to the Parks and Transportation Capital Facilities Plans (CFPs), Impact Fee Facilities Plans (IFFPs), and Impact Fee Analyses (IFAs) for Grantsville City. No formal action will be taken. 

3.	Adjourn



Alicia Fairbourne
Grantsville City Recorder

notices.title: Notice of Ordinance Adoption
notices.agenda_text:
NOTICE OF ORDINANCE ADOPTION

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that at the regular Grantsville City Council Meeting held on April 1, 2026, the Grantsville City Council adopted Ordinance 2026-12, which approves an updated sewer rate schedule to ensure adequate funding for the operation, maintenance, and long-term sustainability of the City's wastewater system, including costs associated with a new wastewater treatment facility. The ordinance adopts a multi-year rate structure through Fiscal Year 2035, which includes increases to residential and non-residential base fees and the implementation of usage-based fees beginning in Fiscal Year 2027. The rate adjustments include higher increases in the initial years followed by smaller annual increases thereafter, and are intended to maintain required financial reserves and meet debt service obligations for the City's wastewater infrastructure.

Complete copies of the adopted ordinances are available for review at the Grantsville City Recorder's office during regular business hours and online through the City's website at https://grantsville.municipalcodeonline.com/book?type=orddoc#name=2026 and the Utah Public Notice Website at https://www.utah.gov/pmn/sitemap/publicbody/1840.html.

Alicia Fairbourne
Grantsville City Recorder

Published and posted on April 7, 2026 in accordance with UCA § 10-3-711

notices.title: Public Notice of Site Visit - Desert Edge Groundbreaking
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE Groundbreaking Ceremony - Possible Quorum of City Council

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that a groundbreaking ceremony is scheduled to take place on Monday, April 6, 2026 at 11:00 a.m. at the Desert Edge Subdivision information Center, located at 1179 West Vegas Street, Grantsville, Utah.

Members of the City Council are invited to attend this event, and a quorum of the City Council may be present. No formal meeting would be convened, and no city business will be acted upon during the open house.

Alicia Fairbourne Grantsville City Recorder

Posted on April 2, 2026 in accordance with the Utah Open and Public Meetings Act.

notices.title: Notice of Public Hearing and Bonds to be Issued
notices.agenda_text:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING AND BONDS TO BE ISSUED

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN pursuant to the provisions of the Local Government Bonding Act, Title 11, Chapter 14, Utah Code Annotated 1953, as amended, (the 'Act'), that on February 4, 2026, the City Council (the 'Council') of Grantsville City, Utah (the 'Issuer'), adopted a resolution (the 'Resolution') in which it authorized the issuance of the Issuer's Sewer Revenue Bonds, Series 2026 (to be issued in one or more series and with such other series or title designation(s) as may be determined by the Issuer) (the 'Bonds') and called a public hearing to receive input from the public with respect to (a) the issuance of the Bonds and (b) any potential economic impact that the Project described herein to be financed with the proceeds of the Bonds may have on the private sector. 

TIME, PLACE AND LOCATION OF PUBLIC HEARING

The Issuer shall hold a public hearing on April 1, 2026, at the hour of 7:00 p.m. at 429 East Main Street, Grantsville, Utah.  The purpose of the hearing is to receive input from the public with respect to (a) the issuance of the Bonds and (b) any potential economic impact that the Project (defined herein) to be financed with the proceeds of the Bonds may have on the private sector.  All members of the public are invited to attend and participate.  

PURPOSE FOR ISSUING THE BONDS 

The Bonds will be issued for the purpose of (a) financing all or a portion of the costs of the construction of improvements to its sewer system and all related improvements (collectively, the 'Project'), (b) funding a deposit to a debt service reserve fund, if necessary, and (c) paying costs of issuance of the Bonds.

PARAMETERS OF THE BONDS

The Issuer intends to issue the Bonds in the aggregate principal amount of not more than Fifty Million Dollars ($50,000,000), to mature in not more than thirty-four (34) years from their date or dates, to be sold at a price not less than ninety-eight percent (98%) of the total principal amount thereof, and shall bear interest at a rate or rates of not to exceed six percent (6%).  The Bonds are to be issued and sold by the Issuer pursuant to the Resolution, including as part of said Resolution a Master Resolution dated as of December 1, 2018 (the 'Original Master Resolution'), as previously supplemented, and as further supplemented by one or more Supplemental Master Resolutions (collectively, the 'Master Resolution') which was before the Council in substantially final form at the time of the adoption of the Resolution and said Supplemental Master Resolution(s) are to be executed by the Issuer in such form and with such changes thereto as shall be approved by the Issuer; provided that the principal amount, interest rate or rates, maturity, and discount of the Bonds will not exceed the maximums set forth above.  The Issuer reserves the right to not issue the Bonds for any reason and at any time up to the issuance of the Bonds.   

REVENUES PROPOSED TO BE PLEDGED

The Bonds are special limited obligations of the Issuer payable from the net revenues of the Issuer's sewer system (the 'Revenues'). 

OUTSTANDING BONDS SECURED BY REVENUES

The Issuer currently has $5,046,000 of bonds outstanding secured by the Revenues.  

OTHER OUTSTANDING BONDS OF THE ISSUER

Additional information regarding the Issuer's outstanding bonds may be found in the Issuer's financial report (the 'Financial Report') at:  https://reporting.auditor.utah.gov/searchreports/s/.  For additional information, including any information more recent than as of the date of the Financial Report, please contact Aspen Clegg, Finance Director (435) 884-4619.

TOTAL ESTIMATED COST OF BONDS 

Based on the Issuer's current plan of finance and a current estimate of interest rates, the total principal and interest cost of the Bonds, if held until maturity, is $98,649,387.
A copy of the Resolution and the Master Resolution are on file in the office of the City Recorder, 429 East Main Street, Grantsville, Utah where they may be examined during regular business hours of the City Recorder from 8:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. Monday through Friday, for a period of at least thirty (30) days from and after the date of publication of this notice.

NOTICE IS FURTHER GIVEN that a period of thirty (30) days from and after the date of the posting of this notice is provided by law during which any person in interest shall have the right to contest the legality of the Resolution, the Master Resolution (but only as it relates to the Bonds) or the Bonds, or any provision made for the security and payment of the Bonds, and that after such time, no one shall have any cause of action to contest the regularity, formality, or legality thereof for any cause whatsoever.
DATED this February 4, 2026. 

/s/ Alicia Fairbourne 	
City Recorder

notices.title: Grantsville City Council Regular Meeting and Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
PUBLIC NOTICE:

The Grantsville City Council will hold a Regular Meeting and Public Hearing on Wednesday, April 1, 2026 in person at 429 East Main Street, Grantsville UT 84029 and electronically via Zoom beginning at 7:00 pm. 

AGENDA AMENDED 3/31/2026 1:40 PM

The agenda is as follows: 

ROLL CALL 

AGENDA: 

1.	Public Comment
2.	Summary Action Items
     a.	Approval of Minutes from the March 25, 2026 City Council Regular Meeting
     b.	Approval of bills
3.	Presentation of a Community Enhancement grant for the Grantsville Fire Department
4.	Consideration of approving Resolution 2026-17 awarding the contract for professional landscape and memorial design services for the Veterans Memorial Park to Fritz Custom Concrete
5.	Public Hearing: Public hearing to receive input on the issuance of sewer revenue bonds to finance wastewater treatment plant improvements
6.	Consideration of approving Resolution 2026-18 awarding the contract for construction of the Grantsville City Wastewater Treatment Plant to VanCon, Inc.
7.	Consideration of approval of Ordinance 2026-12 adjusting the sewer rate in connection with wastewater treatment plant improvements and associated sewer revenue bonds
8.	Consideration of approving Resolution 2026-20 awarding a contract for rodeo stock services to Broken Heart Rodeo Company, LLC
9.	Discussion and possible approval of an additional $10,000 in funding for the Scenic Slopes Bike Track for installation of timing equipment
10.	Consideration of approving Resolution 2026-19 authorizing the dissolution of the Desert Edge Public Infrastructure District Nos. 2-3
11.	Council Reports
12.	Closed session - (Imminent Litigation, Real Estate Negotiations, Personnel)
13.	Adjourn

Alicia Fairbourne
City Recorder

notices.title: Notice of Public Hearing
notices.agenda_text:
NOTICE OF PUBLIC HEARING
GRANTSVILLE CITY

PUBLIC NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that the Grantsville City Council will hold a Public Hearing on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, at 7:00 p.m. at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main St., Grantsville, UT, for the purpose of receiving public input regarding a proposed land exchange between Grantsville City and Dave and Teresa Reed.

Pursuant to the authority granted to municipalities to acquire and dispose of real property, the City Council is considering a proposed land exchange involving certain property owned by Grantsville City and property owned by Dave and Teresa Reed. The proposed exchange involves the transfer of certain real property between the parties. Grantsville City would convey Parcel No. 01-061-0-0070, consisting of approximately (+/-)20.47 acres, to Dave and Teresa Reed. In exchange, Dave and Teresa Reed would convey Parcel No. 01-068-0-0038 and Parcel No. 01-068-0-0055, consisting of approximately (+/-)14.77 acres combined, to Grantsville City.

This meeting will be held electronically via Zoom and in-person at Grantsville City Hall, located at 429 E. Main Street, Grantsville, Utah, 84029. Public comments are welcome and may be made in person or by dialing or logging into Zoom Conferencing using the following:

Meeting URL: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4358843411?omn=89242001869 
Dial-In: 1-253-215-8782   Meeting ID: 435 884 3411

Public comments may also be sent via email to the City Recorder, Alicia Fairbourne at afairbourne@grantsvilleut.gov. All comments will be distributed to the City Council and entered into public record. Written comments must be received prior 12:00 pm (noon) on the day of the meeting in order to be considered. 

Copies of the proposed ordinance and related materials are available for public review at the Grantsville City Offices during regular business hours and as part of the City Council meeting materials posted on the City's official website and the Utah Public Notice Website. 


DATED this 11th day of March, 2026.

Alicia Fairbourne
Grantsville City Recorder
